description: The Mojahedin-e Khalq (MEK), also known as the People's Mojahedin Organization of Iran (PMOI), originated as a splinter group from the [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran). It was co-founded in 1965 by [Mohammad Hanifnejad](/people/mohammad-hanifnejad), [Saeed Mohsen](/people/saeed-mohsen), and [Ali-Asghar Badizadegan](/people/ali-asghar-badizadegan), blending progressive Islam with anti-imperialist politics. After the three founders were executed by the Shah in 1972, [Massoud Rajavi](/people/massoud-rajavi) emerged as leader upon his release in 1979. The MEK participated in the revolution but clashed with [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s regime, organizing the [June 20 uprising](/events/june-20-uprising-1981) and carrying out the 1981 bombing that killed President [Mohammad-Ali Rajai](/people/mohammad-ali-rajai) and Prime Minister [Mohammad-Javad Bahonar](/people/mohammad-javad-bahonar). After a brutal crackdown, the MEK relocated to Iraq under Saddam Hussein's patronage and founded the [National Liberation Army](/orgs/national-liberation-army-of-iran), whose catastrophic [Operation Forough-e Javidan](/events/operation-forough-e-javidan-1988) in 1988 provided a pretext for the [mass executions](/events/mass-executions-1988) of thousands of prisoners. The [NCRI](/orgs/national-council-of-resistance-of-iran), led by [Maryam Rajavi](/people/maryam-rajavi), serves as its political umbrella. The MEK's intelligence network [revealed Iran's secret Natanz nuclear facility](/events/natanz-nuclear-revelation-2002) in 2002. Now based in Albania, it was removed from the US terrorist list in 2012.
editorial_notes: Removed from US terrorist list in 2012. Based in Albania since 2016.
